title: Copy core buildah content into libpod/buildah
body:
Eventually libpod/podman will want to use both commit and build from buildah's code.  And then
in turn, buildah would vendor in libpod for the core content as well.  This will lead to tighter
integration between the projects and less code duplication.

Once this PR is merged, I will vendor in this code to buildah.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body: LGTM. We want to clean this up at some point (swap to using libpod containers internally, move commit into the image library), but this is a good first step to sharing code between buildah and libpod.
Right, this is a lightweight move, try not to break or change much.
I'm not automatically sold on this.  Buildah is already a library.

@nalin IDK if this helps with the sales job or not, but there are a number of things @baude is trying to address by doing this:

1) Allow Podman and Buildah container operations to play more nicely together.
2) It looks like both projects have a lot of code that if not duplicative,is at least very similar.
3) Hopefully build up a library that both Buildah and Libpod can use hopefully helping with 2.
4) Currently Buildah and Libpod would both need to be installed on Atomic Host to let Libpod work.  Would be nice to be able to drop Buildah as a hard requirement in that environment due to space reasons.

nalin is hesitant to do this as he does not see any immediate benefit to buildah.  therefore, we will again vendor in buildah to make podman commit and build work correctly until further discussion can be had.
